text.title: Laravel PHPUnit / Pest Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns observed in real Laravel test suites. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: `tests/Unit/` runs in 18s even though there are 12 tests; profile shows Laravel boot per test.
antipattern.root-cause: Unit test class extends `Tests\TestCase` (which boots Laravel) instead of `PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase`.
antipattern.fix: Switch base class. Reserve `Tests\TestCase` for Feature tests that actually need the framework.

antipattern.symptom: Test asserts no mail was sent, but the user reports receiving the email; CI green, prod broken.
antipattern.root-cause: `Mail::fake()` was called AFTER `$this->postJson(...)`; the real mail dispatched, then the fake started for the assertion phase.
antipattern.fix: Move every `*::fake()` to the top of the test, before the action. Add a Pest lint rule that fails on `*::fake()` appearing below `$this->` HTTP calls.

antipattern.symptom: Response has `"email": null` and the test still passes.
antipattern.root-cause: `assertJsonStructure(['data' => ['email']])` asserts the key exists, not its value.
antipattern.fix: Use `assertJsonPath('data.email', $expected)` for value checks. Reserve `assertJsonStructure` for deep key-presence in dynamic payloads.

antipattern.symptom: Tests pass in isolation, fail when run in a specific order; root cause hides for weeks.
antipattern.root-cause: Static factory state persists across tests; or `setUp` mutates a shared singleton without resetting.
antipattern.fix: Always use `RefreshDatabase` on Feature tests; reset singletons via `$this->app->instance(...)` in `setUp`; avoid static state in factories.

antipattern.symptom: Test asserts `Carbon::now()->diffInSeconds(...) < 1` and flakes 1 in 50 CI runs.
antipattern.root-cause: Test uses real wall-clock time without freezing it.
antipattern.fix: Wrap the action in `$this->travelTo(now())` (Laravel 9+); assert exact equality with the frozen time. Never use `sleep(...)` in tests.
